title: serapias lingua var. excavata
intro: Serapias lingua var. excavata, also known as the yellow-blooming or excavated tongue orchid, is a variant of the Serapias lingua orchid species that is characterized by its yellow flowers and excavated or sunken labellum. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Serapias lingua var. excavata are said to include the treatment of various conditions, such as respiratory issues, skin infections, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a powder or infused in hot water to create a tea, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
